channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Canadian retail giant Loblaw notifies customers of data breach
item.description: Still, out of an abundance of caution, Loblaw says it has automatically logged out all customers from their accounts. Account holders who need to access the company's digital services will have to log...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Iran MOIS Colludes With Criminals to Boost Cyberattacks
item.description: Iranian APTs have long pretended to be cybercriminal groups. Now they're working with actual cybercriminal groups....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: England Hockey investigating ransomware data breach
item.description: England Hockey, the governing body for field hockey in England, is investigating a potential data breach after the AiLock ransomware gang listed it as a victim on its data leak site. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Commercial Spyware Opponents Fear US Policy Shifting
item.description: Rescinded sanctions and reactivated contracts have created confusion about the Trump administration's spyware policy and where it draws the line....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI-generated Slopoly malware used in Interlock ransomware attack
item.description: A new malware strain dubbed Slopoly, likely created using generative AI tools, allowed a threat actor to remain on a compromised server for more than a week and steal data in an Interlock ransomware a...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Rust-Based VENON Malware Targets 33 Brazilian Banks with Credential-Stealing Overlays
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new banking malware targeting Brazilian users that's written in Rust, marking a significant departure from other known Delphi-based malware famili...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hive0163 Uses AI-Assisted Slopoly Malware for Persistent Access in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a suspected artificial intelligence (AI)-generated malware codenamed Slopoly put to use by a financially motivated threat actor named Hive0163. "Alt...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Veeam warns of critical flaws exposing backup servers to RCE attacks
item.description: Data protection company Veeam Software has patched multiple flaws in its Backup & Replication solution, including four critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerabilities. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Why Stryker's Outage Is a Disaster Recovery Wake-Up Call
item.description: The Iranian cyberattack on Stryker is the kind of stress test that business continuity and disaster recovery programs often do not plan for....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: US disrupts SocksEscort proxy network powered by Linux malware
item.description: Law enforcement agencies in the U.S. and Europe along with private partners have disrupted the SocksEscort cybercrime proxy network that used only edge devices compromised via the AVRecon malware for ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google paid $17.1 million for vulnerability reports in 2025
item.description: Google paid over $17 million to 747 security researchers who reported security bugs through its Vulnerability Reward Program (VRP) in 2025. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Telus Digital confirms breach after hacker claims 1 petabyte data theft
item.description: Canadian business process outsourcing giant Telus Digital has confirmed it suffered a security incident after threat actors claimed to have stolen nearly 1 petabyte of data from the company in a multi...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Going the Extra Mile: Travel Rewards Turn into Underground Currency.
item.description: Stolen airline miles are converted into flights and hotel stays, then resold as discounted travel. Flare shows how cybercriminals and underground markets treat loyalty accounts like tradable currency....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Apple patches older iPhones and iPads against Coruna exploits
item.description: ​Apple has released security updates to patch older iPhones and iPads against a set of vulnerabilities targeted in cyberespionage and crypto-theft attacks using the Coruna exploit kit. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How to Scale Phishing Detection in Your SOC: 3 Steps for CISOs
item.description: Phishing has quietly turned into one of the hardest enterprise threats to expose early. Instead of crude lures and obvious payloads, modern campaigns rely on trusted infrastructure, legitimate-looking...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: OAuth Trap, EDR Killer, Signal Phishing, Zombie ZIP, AI Platform Hack & More
item.description: Another Thursday, another pile of weird security stuff that somehow happened in just seven days. Some of it is clever. Some of it is lazy. A few bits fall into that uncomfortable category of “yeah… th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US charges another ransomware negotiator linked to BlackCat attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice charged another former DigitalMint employee for his involvement in an insider scheme in which ransomware negotiators secretly partnered with the BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomw...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Attackers Don't Just Send Phishing Emails. They Weaponize Your SOC's Workload
item.description: The most dangerous phishing campaigns aren’t just designed to fool employees. Many are designed to exhaust the analysts investigating them. When a phishing investigation takes 12 hours instead of five...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apple Issues Security Updates for Older iOS Devices Targeted by Coruna WebKit Exploit
item.description: Apple on Wednesday backported fixes for a security flaw in iOS, iPadOS, and macOS Sonoma to older versions after it was found to be used as part of the Coruna exploit kit. The vulnerability, tracked a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Six Android Malware Families Target Pix Payments, Banking Apps, and Crypto Wallets
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered half-a-dozen new Android malware families that come with capabilities to steal data from compromised devices and conduct financial fraud. The Android malware ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited n8n RCE Bug as 24,700 Instances Remain Exposed
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical security flaw impacting n8n to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of ac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: INC Ransomware Group Holds Healthcare Hostage in Oceania
item.description: Government agencies, emergency clinics, and others in Australia, New Zealand, and Tonga have had serious run-ins with the prolific ransomware outfit....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Xygeni GitHub Action Compromised Via Tag Poison
item.description: Attackers operated an active C2 implant for up to a week and compromised AppSec vendor Xygeni's xygeni/xygeni-action in that time....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: WhatsApp introduces parent-managed accounts for pre-teens
item.description: WhatsApp has begun rolling out parent-managed accounts for pre-teens, allowing parents and guardians to decide who can contact them and which groups they can join. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SQLi flaw in Elementor Ally plugin impacts 250k+ WordPress sites
item.description: An SQL injection vulnerability in Ally, a WordPress plugin from Elementor for web accessibility and usability with more than 400,000 installations, could be exploited to steal sensitive data without a...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch n8n RCE flaw exploited in attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered government agencies on Wednesday to patch their systems against an actively exploited n8n vulnerability. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Medtech giant Stryker offline after Iran-linked wiper malware attack
item.description: Leading medical technology company Stryker has been hit by a wiper malware attack claimed by Handala, an Iranian-linked and pro-Palestinian hacktivist group. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New PhantomRaven NPM attack wave steals dev data via 88 packages
item.description: New attack waves from the 'PhantomRaven' supply-chain campaign are hitting the npm registry, with dozens of malicious packages that exfiltrate sensitive data from JavaScript developers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Researchers Trick Perplexity's Comet AI Browser Into Phishing Scam in Under Four Minutes
item.description: Agentic web browsers that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities to autonomously execute actions across multiple websites on behalf of a user could be trained and tricked into falling prey...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports o...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security
